The team head in to figure just what the heck happened to the ship. Thing is though, there's something aboard the ship that's going to make things a lot scarier... and a lot bigger.

1131.content: OK, so we're going with our typical universe. Alright then, what period of time would you want to do?
1131.title: Our main universe

11311.content:
So, we're heading to the Wild West, then. Or anywhere else if you want to do something other then a Western. So, who are we going to be following?

Cleo Winston: An known bounty hunter traveling the American West, collecting bounties to keep herself and her horse, Shade, fed. A rather tough feat, considering that Cleo weighs over 450 pounds of pure fat. Despite her size, Cleo is still a crack-shot with her Colt and whip. She has a strange disorder that prevents her from losing weight, so she tries to keep her eating in check.

Zebediah and Mary-Anne Colton: A married couple, the Colton's are infamous for "Zebediah Colton's All-Natural Tonics", alleged "elixers" that claim to have all manner of healing properties. In reality, the Colton's are con-artists, and the "Tonics" are just random ingredients mixed together. Their big one allegedly can increase fertility ten-fold, and they do have a way to back it up: Mary-Anne is currently pregnant with 10 children, though she has been pregnant for two years now...
11311.title: Mid to late 1800's

113131.content:
So who do we follow?

Lawerence Caponi:
A private dick whose life gets turned upside down when he meets a certain someone.
113131.title: Main:1930

1131311.content:
"We should burn down the bank on eigth street and see those rats running!" A man's voice spoke out to the crowd of folks who were around him. I coughed a little in the back of this mob, my eyes scanned all of their features. Two emotions,anger and worry,danced like maniacs across their faces.

Children hugged their mothers, mothers hugged their husbands, and those poor suckers hugged their bravado. I could see scruffed shoes and ripped pants and scuffed shirts on all of them. I did spot one woman who put quite a show with her ruined clothing though.

Back to the crowd, their mumurs were like a bubbling cauldron. I could feel them contemplate the words of the fellow who they crescented around. They were unions workers, the hard life they lived were like weight on their physiques. Men bugled with muscles as they gripped their wives.

I don't know why they had decided this place was a good place to meet. It was right outside an old factory, whether they worked here or not was none of my concerns. What I was waiting for was someone else, it was on whim that I chose to watch these folks worry for their own exsistence.

"Maybe they're going to a picnic or some other hullabaloom, god knows why the kids are here. Unless they're going to use them as shields when they storm the banks." I mumbled beneath my breath. A child looked up at me, I waved the kid off before I walked away from this scene.

Around me where steel flowed like rivers were instead a weal of destituion.Lost ones cried "brother,can you spare a dime!" to anyone who gave these shmucks an ear. I ignored them, I rounded an alleyway to spot my man.

It was Frankie Ehrlich, my best friend. Well, okay, more of a buisness partner. He stared at me with a raised eyebrow and spoke in that nasally voice that would've gotten his head clogged in if it was anybody else but me.

"Where have you've been Law? I waited five mintues for you."

"Where have I been? You weren't here for twenty minutes, don't give me that tripe." I shot back. Frankie always going be complain about others and not himself if you don't get on his case.

He huffed before he motined over to his car. I allowed myself into the car, buckled myself in, and waited for us to drive off. Frankie whistled a tune while we drove away from this blasted area.

"Look at our country Law." Frankie spoke. His eyes watched the roads full of sleeping men and women. We passed a bus filled to the brim with families no doubt going west.

"Its been three years and we're still in the dumps."

I glaced at him from across the passenger seat.

"We? Frankie the hell you talking about? 'We' are doing quite well for ourselves."

I refered to our not so secertive dealings; me as a 'private dick' and Frankie's not so legal cabbie service. He shrugged, he continued on with another peep.

We soon hit the Pines, those woodlands that stetched forever. I always felt a little colder here, I knew what The Mob did in these forests. And would do my best not to be another esteemed guest here if I willed it.

"So why didn't 'Shiner' just tell me how to get here,Frankie?"

"Thinks that the Coppers have a tail on you." He said with a causal shrug. My eyes shot upwards.

"Bull. You know I don't do anything to piss the blues off. Is that why I was told to do all that hubub back in town?"

"Maybe. In anycase don't do anything to make the guy angry Law. He's already crossed with you as is with that 'cop' thought in his head. Not mad enough to deliver a lead bird into your noggin bu-"

"Yeah, yeah." I huffed out.

Stefan 'Shiner' Gagliano, though I think he took the last name up, was a made man for one of the Five Families. Well thought of among those folks, hell, rumor has it he talked regualrly with Marazano before the old man got whacked. And by luck or god's will, he managed to survive Castellammarese.

But York got too hot for him and he's been out here ever since. He's got his own crew of sixty-five and been cozying it up with the underworld here. Still I think he isn't comfortable with the flavour of crime that this town's got.

Maybe that's why for the last two months he's been renovating this old lumber mill outside of town? Didn't want to be so close to so many august people.

I put this thought away as we neared the encampment. About thirty men were around here, the others were probably back at the old place. I shifted feeling a little worried at the sight of all their guns. Frankie stopped infront of a pair of goons and said a few words.

Finished we drove in and parked ourselves in this makeshift lot. We both got out and Frankie asked a few words to an attendant. I wondered how one got the chauffer rotation.

Frankie came back over, he seemed already in the know about the place. He lead me to the biggest structure in the lumbermill.

With a knock on the door, a goon opened and politely led us in. It seemed that Shiner turned this place into a residance, what with his faux-tiger skin rug and couch. Although it could've been a real tiger he had skinned that we walked upon.

Still this was an office at one point and no matter how you dressed it up, It felt like I was going to meet my boss who was going to punch my pinkslip.

"I'll try to greese Shiner up. You stay here." Frankie spoke. He walked off into a far off room leaving me with the goon. I should've used a softer word though because the guy wasn't too ape-like compared to the other men around here. He belonged on the silver screen or maybe he did belong when I got a closer look at his biceps.

I thought the next few minutes were going to be a bore until the most beautiful gal walked out with a creek of the wood underneath her.

"Rudolph, I got another hiccup on the budget can yo-" She stopped when she saw me and I gaped as I saw her.

She had a merticulous curled brown hair of an Uptown girl. Her eyes as green as the a spring leaf.She wore little makeup on her face, but she probably didn't need any in the first place. Red lips were curled into a frown yet that did little to hide her beauty.

Her body was covered by a equally green dressed, though not as vibrant as her eyes. My eyes on the other hand were drawn towards her hips.

They were out of this world, literally.

With the material on it they did little to hide my imagination. These hips wider than her shoulders and then some. They were unaturally large things that I didn't even know could exsist.

With an almost shy peek I could see the faint outline of a rump.

We stared at one another for minutes on end,both caught in this moment.
1131311.title: Lawerence Caponi

choices: "...check out a robbery at one of his places."
11313111.content:
"Oh. I take it you are Mr. Caponi?" The woman asked.

I nodded, eyes still drawn to the woman's hips. The woman seemed to notice, and scowled.

"Mr. Gagliano is currently occupied." The woman said.

"I'm aware." I replied. "There anywhere I can sit so I can wait for him?"

The woman nodded and motioned for me to follow her. She then turned and went into one of the doors. I now saw just how large her backside seemed to be.

Each cheek was easily bigger then the woman's head, and gave her entire rear a heart shape. Every single step the woman took made her butt wobble and shake, made more obvious with the fact that her hips swung nearly a foot with every step. It was quite a display.

The woman led me to some kind of waiting room outside of Gagliano's office. She motioned for me to take a seat. I did so, and she went back to the folder she had been carrying the entire time.

There was a silence in the room as I sat waiting, the woman flipping through the folder every now and then. Finally, she decided to break the silence.

"I know you were staring." She said. "I don't exactly blame you. You don't see a woman like me around."

"Do you mind if I asked how?" I asked.

She stayed silent at that fact.

I was about to ask her what she did for Gagliano when the office door opened, and Frankie stepped out.

"Alright, Gagliano says he'll give us a job." Frankie said, before turning and seeing the woman. "Well, hello there, miss..."

"Betty." The woman replied.

"Frankie, what's Gagliano want?" I asked, pulling Frankie's attention.

"Oh, right. Gagliano wants us to..."
11313111.title: Betty

choices: The duo meet Young Chinese Kent, the son of Chinese Kent
Law gathers evidence, no need for the conversation
113131113.content:
"One of his places were hit, you gotta go see to it." Frankie stated.

He gave a cough while Betty moved away to allow us some room. Which wasn't that much, weird though, big burly men were supposed to lounge here. It could've been a scare tactic to make them seem smaller than they were, I guess the people who built this place didn't have a woman with a caboose like Betty. Anywho, while I made my way out I could see the woman disappear back to her corner office, a flash of hip was the last sight of her before we moved away.

I wiped my forward in relief, the place got a lot hotter when she came into the room. I was having doubts that the woman was even real and I imagined a large hipped lass from my lustful daydreams. I spat out in front of me which I realized soon after was a mistake once I stepped back into it.

"Didja know about that lass, Frankie? You'd have to pay a quarter to see her in a roadside attraction." I told the man. We marched back to our cars, Frankie whistled over to the chauffer for our keys back. With a toss the man gave our keys back. I gave a nod to the guy while we stepped back into the vehicle. A twist of his keys in the ignition brought our car back to life.

Frankie either had his tongue tied or wanted a calmer room to talk back to me.

"I only heard her voice the last time I was here,Law. Didn't even know all about what she carried in the back." Frankie said with a smile. I was both relived and shocked that I didn't formulate that Fata Morgana.Those cheeks were out of this world, a imposition of a Freudian world on our normal reality. I still was reeling from the sight that was becoming memory.

"Do women like that exsist everywhere,Frankie? Who was she?"

"Betty, she told us, Law." He stuck his tongue out at me. I huffed back.

"No smartass, whose she in Shiner's organization?"

"Hell if I know, Law. Does it really matter, it's not like its your job to know is it?" Frankie cruised down the road with one hand on the wheel and the other pointing at me. "What is your job is to look into the robbed place, that's important to Gagliano, don't you forget."

I sighed in response. "So what's this place called?"

"The name's 'Chinese Kent's Wishy-wash' it got hit about a day ago. The Blue's already got their fill but Gagliano want's you to show your magic."

"There's Chinese here, what about the good old NYC. The hell they doing in Jersey?" I questioned.

"Fugg if I know, but they're paying the mob for protection. And they weren't protected." Frankie answered. "So get your head in the game and go fix it."

Our car drove in silence until we reached our destination. A young Chinese man ran his hand through his head until he saw our car pooling up. Frankie waved followed by his thumb going up.
113131113.title: Law: A Robbed Place

choices: Law doesn't find anything to note
1131311131.content:
The young man gave us a wave while we approached, sweat dripped down his brow. I could tell he hadn't slept for awhile the evident bags under his eyes. Frankie gave the man a handshake while he introduced himself.

"Chinese Kent?" Frankie asked.

"No that's my father, Kent the Younger or Kent Jr." The Chinese man replied. "Are you going to help with my problem?"

"With the 'thing', right?" Frankie asked. He pointed behind the man. The Chinese man looked over at Frank with a raised eyebrow.

"Oh course, wha- nevermind. So whose the dick behind you?"

"Lawerence Caponi, pleased to meet you." I hastily introduced myself to the young man. He nodded before he led us inside the store. On the outside it was pretty clean, didn't even look to be the source of a robbery. Inside thought was another story, place looked like the aftermath of Dresden. With busted open washers and more debris than I could count.

"Did you know why you were hit?" I asked Kent Jr.

"Ask my dad when he comes back, he's at the police station right now." Kent Jr replied. He guided past areas with marked debris, no doubt to clean it later. We came to the back office, or at least, what I thought was the back office. Place was more destroyed than our previous arena. I coughed from the dust in the air.

"Going to look around Mr. Caponi?" Kent Jr questioned. I nodded with a quick shake. Frankie behind me left with the Kent while I went to work. I carefully stacked whatever was light and loose into neat piles.

"Alright, Law, do your thing." I said to myself.
1131311131.title: Law: Young Chinese Kent

choices: 1930's-1940's: Depression and Then the 2nd greatest war
1950's: When Rock was the smooth Elvis.
1132.title: A strange alternative world

11322.content:
OK, so we're somewhere between 1930 and 1949 for our story. Exactly who are we going to be following, and what makes this world different from our own?

Patricia Delacroix: A private detective currently living in downtown LA. She's a hard-boiled private-eye with a love of booze and women. In her world, all women have larger breasts then normal: an H cup is the minimum size.

Roger Coffey: A soldier who's currently fighting in one of the biggest wars on the planet. This war is made worse by the fact that in his world, people are able to change into various elements, making battle much more difficult. All he wants to do is go home to his wife and daughter.
11322.title: 1930's-1940's

choices: Jenny comes in with another client.
Patricia accepts the case, unofficially, no criminal connection
113221.content:
I listened in on my radio as it played out a ritzy jazz tune. I tapped the side of my desk as the rhythm made my mind wander. Soon my eyes closed and I drifted off into a tranquil state. A knock at my door alerted me back to reality. My aide, Jenny, came with a nicely dressed man. Which was a rarity considering what the rest of the country was like. Hell, considering what the world was like.

He coughed for a moment and looked down at me. Surprisingly not my boobs though, which to be fair, weren't that big compared to other women. Anyways, back onto track, I looked over the man's face waiting for what he said next.

"Are you Patricia Delacroix?" He told me. I smiled, with one hand I pointed out my name plate in gold. Which was just painted that way, I wouldn't keep actual gold in the presence of others in this day and age.

"At your service, what can I do for you?" I asked. I shook his hand which made my boobs wobble for a bit. It smacked down against my desk, but my extra strength bra did its job. He sat himself down on a chair and began to discuss his woes.

"My name is Dan Mackenzie an-"

I interrupted his sentence to put in my own observations. "Mackenzie? Any relations to Lisa Mackenzie?"

"Nephew." The poor boy blushed at that. I groaned though at his words, internally of course. The Mackenzie's were the biggest crime family in the upper northside of the city. They beat back the mafia even when the Italians brought in major firepower. Even when the mafia lit two blocks on fire. It was bloody but the Irish still stood tall. I tried to remember if Dan was one of those who stood beside Lisa, but nothing came to light.

I knew she had many nieces and nephews for the life of me though I couldn't find a Dan in my mind. I'd sort that out later as I refocused my attention onto my client.

"So what do you need?"

Dan slid a piece of paper across to me, it was a photo of a diamond ring with intricate spirals. Reminded me of an Oriental vase or various other riches of that like. I looked up at Dan to let him explain himself.

"It's a ring." Was all he stated either he got tongued tied or he wanted the obvious pointed because he didn't say anything for awhile until I spoke.

"I thought it was an amulet, I thank you for the clarification. What's so special about it?"

"It's the ring Auntie took from Chinese Lance. It represents the unity of our factions in the face of the mob."

I nodded at his words. I always had an inkling that the Chinese helped the Irish when the Mafia came rushing into their turf. I just never had the curiosity to see it thoroughly investigated. Odd that he was telling me this, unless...

"What happened to it?"

"I...I lost it." Dan said his words like they were a pain to say. I blinked at him in surprise.

"Why'd you have it in the first place?"

"My auntie asked my mom for a favor; we aren't part of the business, so I was told to keep it. Yeah we have the same name as auntie, but my mom makes sure that none of us are seen with her. So I guess it fell to us to keep the thing all nice and tucked away. I was supposed to keep it for a few days, I don't know why auntie's men couldn't just have it in their hideaways. Everything was fine, that was until yesterday I got my home broken into and found out that the ring's gone missing." Dan explained.

"So have you've gone to the cops yet?" I asked. He shook his head. Smart guy, I knew at least six cops were on the mob's payroll so If this got out it would've been a disaster.

"Why'd you come to me anyways?"

"Auntie's said you were good people." Dan answered. I felt touched at that, if not for the fact Lisa was a goddamned kingpin. "Look, I'll pay you seventy-five of what my aunt gave to me for keeping the ring if you'd help out. All in cash."

The poor guy looked destitute at the thought of failing his aunt. If it was anyone else I probably would've helped, but an idea popped into head. I could just deny the looking for the ring and let it rot, it's not like I had any love for the Chinese underworld and Irish here. Let them duke it over this fancy ring, which I wondered why it was so important besides it representing their hand-holding. At the same time, the mafia could come in and take what was left of this city. Well besides the docks at any rate.

Choices, choices.
113221.title: Patricia Delacroix

1132212.content:
I sighed. I had a good feeling that if I didn't help to get that ring back, Dan here wouldn't see another sunrise.

"OK, I'll accept you case." I said. Dan beemed at that.

"Thank you so much!" Dan replied. "I'll get the money as soon as I can. Just get that ring back!"

"Does anyone in your aunt's circle know the ring is missing?" I asked.

"Everyone." Dan replied, upset. "Nothing stays hidden from auntie. She's giving me 3 days to get that ring back, or I'm going back home in a pine box!"

"Dan, I need you to calm down." I said. "Just point me in the direction of someone I can talk to, and I can get to work on finding that ring."
-----------
Once calm, Dan told me to check his house first. It was in a suburb, identical to the houses around it. So identical, you'd never know he lived there. So whoever snagged that ring must've known him.

As I strolled through the neighborhood, keeping an eye out for the address Dan gave me, I let my eye wander around. I saw quite a few people walking about, chatting or doing chores.

It was hard keeping my eyes off some of the women. I'd known for a fair while I liked girls instead of guys, so I sometimes felt my gaze shift to their bosoms. Some of them were just H cups, some were about my size, and some were much larger. Hell, one woman had a pair that probably could fill a couch.

Didn't see many men around. Probably shipped off overseas. Probably at work. Who knew. Hope they get to see home again. Lost my daddy in Saint-Mihiel, and I pray I don't lose my brother.

I found the house. Mostly from the people around it. Mostly from the landmarks. And mostly because the place was currently up in flames. Firefighters had the blaze mostly under control.

I managed to find the one in charge of the firefighters here. Hopefully, he'll give me something. I get his attention and give him my name.

"Delacroix? The detective?" The firefighter asked. "One of the guys at the station talked about how you found his sister. Guessin' your here 'cause of the blaze?"

"That'd be right." I replied. "I've got a client. This was his house."

"Well, you ain't finding anything useful here." The firefighter said. "Someone set fire in the basement. Whole inside's looking like a firepit at a campground. Almost everything's ashes."

"Almost everything?" I asked.

"Someone left a matchbook. Probably started the damn blaze." The firefighter said, before showing me the matchbook. The front read...
1132212.title: The Case is On

choices: One of the patrons seems to be acting weird right now, Pat sees
Pat allows herself out and is attacked
Pat waits to for the night to come to investigate Dan's home
11322121.content:
The Broken Vault. The pub? But that was owned by the Irish, why use it? I rubbed my chin in thought before I tipped my hat to the firefighter before I handed the evidence back to him.

"Are you going to make sure you won't tell the coppers?" I spoke. The man smiled and waved me off. Always could trust the fire brigade if the cops aren't doing well. With a whistle I walked over to the bus stop. Hey, don't blame me for my transportation, blame how hard it is to acquire a car for a gal like myself!

After fifteen minutes, with my boobs wobbling, I arrived to The Broken Vault. Damn these things sometimes, when they can make a ride not feel like a ship, then our worlds will truly be a paradise. Anyhow I stared at the scenery. The place was a converted bank, at one point it held a speakeasy for all the Irish. And you know the Irish with their alcohol, a fish would envy their liquid cupidity. In an almost ironic twist, it made more dough than its white collar acquisitions.

Doubly ironic, when the thirtieth was repealed, it went public with it's pub. And when the depression hit like a truck, the alcohol still flowed stronger than the green. Still, the I think the alcohol helps deal with life's stress at the moment.

Their wasn't a bouncer at the door, maybe that was out of question for their current budget. I stepped right in, the bank bars had been widened to allow easier movement between drink and patron. Which weren't that many, a few sat on torn open chairs or sat on the floor. I saw a familiar face greet me.

"Hello, Pat." A Irish voice called out. "What brings you here?"

I walked over to a mustached man named Curly, not the Stooge though. Curly was a mid-ranking member of Lisa's family; it could also be an organization or maybe alliance? It wasn't like the mob's clear cut hierarchy, it was made up of over twelve families with the Mackenzie's being the main driving family. At any rate Curly controlled not only the pub, but also the importation of alcohol. I knew the coppers did their best, at the mob's behest, to choke his control out, but Curly persevered.

"Hey Curly, I found your matchstick at the scene of a crime."

His eyes crossed for a moment, with one finger he pointed me to a door to allow myself across the bars. Once I did so, I walked with the man until we entered the vault. The vault had be converted into the place's alcohol stronghold. The other patrons didn't seem to mind my special treatment; either they were too drunk or just didn't care.

"Talk, Pat."

"It was found at Dan Mackenzie's house. Nephew of Lisa."

He released an uncouth 'fahck' and mumbled about Lisa's retribution. I knew she didn't like people messing with her family, even if they were marginally connected. Though I think poor Dan had reached her limits seeing as he had lost the ring that was really important. I didn't know if Curly knew and I didn't feel to tell him.

"Listen Curly, did you see any seedy types who wanted to burn down a house that belonged to a Mackenzie?"

"You mean besides everybody who comes into my pub? No." He said with a shrug. "I don't keep track of my matchbooks, Pat. He could've just got it from off the street for all I know."

I sighed, that could be a possibility. So I was back to square one now. And with the burned out wreck of Dan's house, I would have to wait for the literal and metaphorical heat to die down. The cops would be there soon and I didn't want the mafia to be alerted.
11322121.title: ...The Broken Vault.

11323.content:
Alrighty then we're somewhere between 1950 and 1959 for our story. Now then which character's story will we follow and what will be different about this alternative world?

Ace Dalmatian: He happens to be a well known celebrity currently living in San Francisco California as he's the star of an animated show known as Dog Daring guardian of the galaxy, as our hero's about to be fattened up by some evil aliens themselves in an upcoming episode of this classic 50's era animated show.

Doug Dalmatian: He happens to be a well known pro wrestler from Pittsburgh Pennsylvania as he's about to appear on a show known as Studio Wrestling but first he has to gain plenty of weight before he makes his big debut.
11323.title: 1950's: When Rock was the smooth Elvis.

115.content: So where shall it be?
115.title: Another place...

13.content:
Where do we start out?

20 Minutes in the Future: Like Batman Beyond

Cyberpunk: Does it really need to be said what this is?

Space Opera: Colony spanning civilization

Post-Apocalyptic: After the fall.

One Step Beyond: The first years of the new space age
13.title: Futuristic

134.content: Yikes. So the whole planets gone to crap, eh? Well, we need to figure something out about that: what CAUSED the fall of humanity.
134.title: Post-Apocalyptic

1342.content:
So it's happened. Thanks to a viral outbreak, the Earth's deceased are roaming the planet again, and the living are fighting for survival. So, who in this zombie apocalypse are we following?

Clara Becker: A bookstore employee in her 30's who was leaving work when the outbreak happened. She, unfortunately, got bitten, but for some reason did not turn. No, the virus instead had another effect on her: her butt is continually growing bigger and bigger. Now she's just fighting for survival, hoping to find a safe haven before she's eaten... or before her butt becomes too heavy to move.

Julia Drake: Much like Clara, 29 year old Julia was bitten by one of the zombies during the initial outbreak. This caused her to mutate into what the people call a "unique": a type of special zombie. In her case, she turned into a "Sinker", a zombie whose become bloated with infected water. Amazingly, she somehow managed to avoid losing her mind, leaving her as a Sinker zombie with human intelligence.
1342.title: Zombie virus outbreak

13421.content:
The highway was completely abandoned, save for a few empty cars strewn here and there. That was fine by Clara. If there wasn't a living soul on the highway, it also meant she didn't have to deal with any of the undead.

Right now, Clara was walking down said highway, carrying a backpack full of supplies, a flare gun and a few remaining flares, and a length of pipe. It wasn't much, but for the moment it was the best Clara had to keep herself safe. All she had on was a grey tank top and a pair of jeans. The Korean-American looked further down the highway, and was frustrated to see she was nowhere close to her destination.

Sighing, she looked to one of the abandoned cars. She hoped it still had it's key in the ignition, but sadly, it did not. She was at least able to locate a map of the surrounding area.

She took a quick look at the map, and set her hand on her hip... and was dismayed to find her hand met her hip sooner then she'd hope.

"Dammit, grew again." Clara muttered.

When the outbreak had first occurred, Clara actually had been bitten, but somehow she didn't turn. No, the virus had a different effect on her: a big booty. She had no idea why, but over the months, her butt had grown from a firm rear, to the kind of bubble butt seen in songs all about big butts. She hated its size, how it jiggled with every step she took, and how it made putting on her jeans an annoyance.

Frustrated, she looked at the map in hopes of finding someplace she could at least spend the night. Thankfully, there was a motel just off the upcoming exit. She could rest there, then make her way to her destination.

Stuffing the map in her backpack, along with a few supplies that hadn't been stolen from the car, and started walking to the highway exit.
---------
It hadn't taken long for Clara to find the motel, but getting in was another story. It was abandoned like the cars, and the owner had already turned into a zombie, and turned the other guests. Thankfully for Clara, they were mostly normal zombies, save for one Slicer. She managed to make quick work of them, doing her damn best to avoid another bite, and managed to find a room that hadn't been occupied.

Taking the keys off the owner, Clara entered the motel room, and threw her backpack on the bed. She turned to see if the bathroom had running water, but turned too fast, and knocked over the TV with her ass. It crashed on the ground. Quickly, Clara looked out the motel room windows. Thankfully, no other zombies were in the area.

"Stupid ass!" Clara yelled as she angrily slapped her enlarged rear, making it wobble. Sighing, she decided to check her map again before seeing if there was running water. She didn't know it's exact location, all she knew was that she really needed to get to a...
13421.title: Clara Becker

134213.content:
A factory, that would be the best place to bunker down in. Especially a steel mill or a automobile place. It would be confusing for both the zombies and anybody who tried to rob her. Bandits had been spotted around the area, if what that last trader she met had said was true. She brushed off her butt while she thought of the next place to go.

"Maybe it's so big because of my diet?" She mumbled. She wasn't alway like this, she had the famous Asian curvature before all this shit happened. That is to say her body wasn't nothing to write home about. Hell, it wasn't nothing to even note. But she had yet to figure out why this was happening to her. Maybe it had a correlation to her other problem. With a sigh, she sat down on her tuckus to see her leg.

The scar was still there, the faint impression of a bite could be seen. She rubbed at it gently with a grimace. The pain of the event flashed in briefly her mind. Another sigh came from within her. She laid down on the motel's bed, her butt sprawled out.

With a turn of her neck she gasped to see evening come like a stallion. Faint howls could be heard off in the distance and they weren't from wolves. Clara cried out before she caught herself. She stalked over to the door cracking it open for a peek. There, in the parking lot, one could see the shambling forms of the undead. What's worse she could see larger, sleeker, or damn well meaner looking silhouettes darting through the town.

With a curse, she pushed the door shut, closed the window, and looked for a barricade. Luckily for her she seemed to have found it the cabinet that held the TV display on it. One stride over and Clara began to push her salvation forward. A rip, which she didn't feel like investigating at the moment, echoed throughout the room. The woman took careful movements to arrange the cabinet in such a way that it wedged itself if the door was knocked down.

She wiped her head with a sigh right until she could feel cold air on her backside. Looking behind her Clara cried out with scorn when she realized her pants had ripped.

"Stupid butt, why are you getting bigger?" The Korean woman grumbled.
134213.title: ...factory.

1342133.content:
Before Clara even had a chance to shut her eyes, she heard a series of gunshots coming from outside the hotel. Instantly jumping up, she ran to the window and saw two people about her age shooting at a group of zombies. Immediately, she grabbed the nearest thing she could use as a weapon, and ran out of the room.

Down in the motel's parking lot, the two were doing their best to keep the zombies at bay. One of them, a woman, was blasting at zombies with a pistol, while the other, a man, was lying on the ground bleeding, trying to keep an SMG steady and aimed at the undead. The two were doing alright, but the zombies kept coming.

Instantly, Clara appeared next to them and threw her improvised weapon, a lamp, at one of the zombies. It staggered the zombie, letting the woman shoot it in the head. Thankfully, it seemed that was the last zombie. Sighing, the woman turned to Clara.

"Thanks. Don't know where you came from." The woman said.

"I was up in one of the rooms, about to finally catch some sleep." Clara replied. She then looked at the man. "What happened?"

"Some of those fast zombies jumped us from the woods." The woman said. "Oh, right. I'm Irene, and that's Deacon."

"Clara." Clara replied, before looking at Deacon again. "Did he... get bitten?"

"No, thank god." Irene said. "Sliced his side on a broken piece of metal." Irene then looked at Clara's arm, and saw the old bite mark. "Oh god, you..."

Clara looked down, and realized what Irene was referring to.

"Oh, that. That actually happened near the beginning of the outbreak." Clara replied. "For some reason, I didn't turn."

"You're... immune?" Deacon finally spoke up, weak from blood loss.

"Not completely. The bite caused... this." Clara said, before turning around and show the two her inflated rear.

"Geez, you could balance a glass on that." Irene said. Deacon was about to say something, but winced in pain from his wound.

"We need to patch him up, fast!" Irene said, immediately switching focus. "Does this place have any first-aid kits?"

Clara quickly started running to the office to look for a first aid kit. And she was able to find...
1342133.title: Survivors

info.brief_description: For all of your expansion and transformative ideas.
info.description:
A experimental interactive where one gets a glimpse at a variety of expansion ideas that are too out of line for other interactives. Come and enjoy the view. Note though keep all additions to a minimum of 1100 words and have at least basic knowledge about spelling.

Allowed:
All forms of inflation and expansion
Stretching and Flattening.
Shrinking
TG
Species Change.
Element Change

Not-Allowed:
Underage
Scenes of extreme gore. Extreme violence is allowed though keep it to a superhero level.
Scat/Waterworks that are not fruit juice.
Pure pornography.
info.pretty_title: What A Large World: A Fetish Interactive
